patches.rationale: SEC-6.4 curmudgeon Cycle 2: 70% is not 'nearly three-quarters' (75%). A 5-percentage-point overstatement is exactly the kind of numerical imprecision we criticize the dome model for. Change to the precise figure the template will compute.

patches.find: registered on 2026-04-05 — hours before this critical review was launched — a batch expansion of the WIN count using well-established constants that add no discriminating power. Strategic timing: adopting nineteenth-century tidal constants as new "predictions" on the eve of an independent audit raises the question of whether these WINs were added to inflate the count rather than because the dome model genuinely predicts them.
patches.replace: registered on 2026-04-05 — a batch expansion of the WIN count using well-established constants that add no discriminating power regardless of when they were added.
patches.rationale: SEC-6.4 curmudgeon Cycle 2: motive attribution ('hours before this review was launched') is an ad hominem that weakens an otherwise strong factual argument. We don't know whether the timing was deliberate. The factual argument stands without it: the tidal WINs are derived from 19th-century constants and add no discriminating power.

patches.find: The dome model has no galactic-scale mechanism — it describes a local cavity ~20,000 km across. Claiming a cosmological distance-redshift relation from a dome model that cannot see past its own firmament is a category error. The formula is borrowed, not derived.
patches.replace: The dome claims an aetheric redshift mechanism, but this produces λ_A = c/H₀ = 4,283 Mpc — numerically identical to standard cosmology's Hubble length — because it uses the standard value H₀ = 70 km/s/Mpc. The dome model does not derive H₀ from dome geometry; it imports the globe-cosmology value and relabels the cause. The formula is borrowed, not derived.
patches.rationale: SEC-6.4 curmudgeon Cycle 2: saying the dome 'has no galactic-scale mechanism' is slightly wrong — it claims an aetheric redshift mechanism. The stronger, accurate argument is that the mechanism produces numerically identical results because it imports the standard H₀ value rather than deriving it from dome parameters.
